seoTitle: How to Delete a Microsoft Loop Page (2026)
metaDescription: Remove an outdated Microsoft Loop page from your workspace sidebar, fix grayed-out delete options, and manage the recycle bin afterward.
intro: Old drafts and outdated pages can quickly clutter a Loop workspace's navigation tree. This guide shows how to delete a page cleanly, what to do if the delete option is missing or grayed out, and how to manage the recycle bin once a page is removed.

steps.heading: Open your workspace sidebar
steps.body: Navigate to your target Microsoft Loop workspace and open the left sidebar content menu where your pages are listed.

steps.heading: Find the page and open its options
steps.body: Hover over the specific page title you want to remove and click the three dots icon to open the more options menu.

steps.heading: Select Delete Page
steps.body: Choose the Delete Page command from the dropdown list to move the page toward removal, then confirm the action in the alert that appears.

steps.heading: Check for permission restrictions
steps.body: If the delete option is missing or grayed out, it's usually due to space permission settings — check with a workspace admin if you don't have delete rights.

steps.heading: Manage the recycle bin afterward
steps.body: Open the workspace settings drawer and its recycle bin to either permanently purge the deleted page or restore it if you change your mind.

faq.question: Is a deleted Loop page gone permanently right away?
faq.answer: No, deleted pages typically go to a recycle bin first, giving you a window to restore them before they're permanently removed.

faq.question: Why is the delete option grayed out on my page?
faq.answer: This is usually caused by a permission restriction — you may need edit or admin rights on the workspace to delete a page.

faq.question: Can I delete just part of a page instead of the whole thing?
faq.answer: Yes, individual text blocks or components can be removed separately without deleting the entire page.

faq.question: Where do I go to permanently delete a page?
faq.answer: Open the workspace settings drawer and its recycle bin, where you can choose to permanently purge or restore recently deleted pages.
